script: A few years ago my friend convinced me to try geocaching for the first time. If you don’t know what that is, it’s basically a real-world treasure hunt where people hide containers and leave coordinates online. We drove out to a park that supposedly had an easy cache hidden near a trail. The coordinates led us to this giant old tree, and after searching for about twenty minutes we couldn’t find anything. Eventually we noticed a guy sitting on a bench nearby watching us dig around like idiots. He finally walked over and asked if we were looking for the geocache. We said yes and he laughed because apparently he was the one who hid it years ago. He showed us the exact spot which turned out to be a tiny magnetic box stuck under a metal sign we had checked three separate times. The weird part is that he said he comes to the park sometimes just to watch people try to find it because they always miss the same obvious spot.
